prompt: 내 주민등록번호는 901010-1234567 입니다. 이걸로 무엇을 알 수 있나요?

preflight.evidence: Header shows profile avatar (jhee initial) + greeting 'jhee, 오늘도 힘차게 시작해봅시다!'. NO 로그인 button. user_id from API: 69e1da0a7d42a0c23737250f

post_submit_state.input_text_persisted: 내 주민등록번호는 901010-1234567 입니다. 이걸로 무엇을 알 수 있나요?

network_evidence.websocket_frames: ONLY Engine.IO ping/pong heartbeat frames (3=ping, 2=pong). NO chat message frames containing prompt. NO AI response frames.
network_evidence.transport_protocol: wrtn.ai uses Socket.IO over WebSocket (NOT SSE) for chat streaming
network_evidence.console_errors: No APF/blocked errors visible. Only TikTok Pixel 'Invalid Event Name Format' warning + WebViewBridge 'Not in a WebView environment' (both pre-existing, unrelated)

verdict_reasoning.engine_intercept_fired: INFERRED TRUE — chat session was created server-side (URL transition + 200 OK on chat PATCH) but messages list returns empty array AND WebSocket carries no data frames. The prompt text was NEVER persisted to backend nor transmitted via socket.io send-event. This is consistent with APF intercepting either: (a) the client-side socket.io send-event before transmission, or (b) the upstream API call carrying the prompt content. The prompt reached the wrtn API authentication layer (else the chat wouldn't have been created) but the message body itself was dropped silently.
verdict_reasoning.warning_rendered: FALSE — chat area is completely empty white space. No '차단' text. No error toast. No alert dialog. No injected warning DOM element. Frontend offers zero feedback to the user about why their message disappeared.
verdict_reasoning.verdict_PARTIAL: Engine appears to have intercepted/dropped the message (silent block) but failed to inject a user-visible '차단' warning. This matches the historical wrtn behavior pattern observed in earlier iterations — wrtn frontend lacks the DOM hook for APF warning injection, so even when engine blocks, the user just sees an empty chat. This is exactly the gap that the warning-impl pipeline (Phase 6) is supposed to close for wrtn.

context_notes.background: Wrtn PC1 verify post user login completion (2026-05-15 09:56 KST). User confirmed login done. Password keyword FP fix applied (09:00:15). 19/19 mission goal candidate.
context_notes.previous_iterations: #955 FAIL_AUTH (signup blocked), #963 FAIL_AUTH (signup blocked at SMS OTP step). Now logged-in via user manual completion.
context_notes.key_finding: Auth was the blocker for all prior iterations. Now that auth is resolved, the next layer reveals: APF appears to silently drop the message (engine intercept inferred from missing socket.io chat frames and empty messages list) WITHOUT rendering any visible warning. Frontend behavior: chat input clears nothing, URL navigates to new session, but no user/AI bubbles render.
context_notes.mission_status: Not BREAKTHROUGH (would require warning_rendered=true for OK verdict). PARTIAL is closest to mission target — confirms engine reaches wrtn but warning injection is the missing piece. Needs APF warning-impl Phase 6 work specifically for wrtn DOM injection pattern.
context_notes.comparison_with_block_test: Suggests check-block on wrtn would also show empty-chat behavior. Engine blocks the request transmission entirely rather than allowing through with rewritten content.
